full_prompt: Design the primary load-bearing chassis frame of a lunar-analog rover benchmark, competing in the all-tasks configuration that combines the Mapping, Excavation, and Science missions. The chassis must host six independently-driven wheel modules on a rocker-bogie suspension, a front-mounted excavation tool mount able to transmit a 500 N horizontal digging reaction force, and a top-deck payload interface plate. Total rover wet mass including payload and excavation regolith must not exceed 90 kg for the combined all-tasks configuration (50 kg for Mapping-only and 60 kg for Excavation-only sub-configurations must also remain feasible by removing bolt-on payload). The structure is manufactured from 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson ratio 0.33, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3). The chassis must fit within a stowed envelope of 1.2 m length x 0.9 m width x 0.8 m height with suspension retracted. Load cases: (LC1) 4 g vertical shock on all four outer wheel mounts representing curb drop, with 90 kg total mass supported; (LC2) excavation reaction 500 N horizontal plus 200 N vertical applied at the tool-mount bolt pattern while the six wheel contact patches are pinned; (LC3) slope traversal at 30 degree pitch with 1 g gravity and 90 kg mass; (LC4) lateral tip load 300 N at the top-deck payload center-of-mass at 0.5 m height above the wheel plane. Pass/fail: maximum von Mises stress across all load cases must remain below 184 MPa (276 MPa / 1.5 ultimate FoS), maximum chassis deflection at payload deck must remain below 3 mm, first natural frequency of the bare chassis must exceed 30 Hz to avoid coupling with drive-motor excitation, and chassis dry mass must remain below 35 kg to preserve mass budget for drivetrain, avionics, and payload. Deliverables: STEP AP242 solid model, FEA report with stress and deflection per load case, modal analysis for the first five eigenmodes, and a mass budget summary.
short_prompt:
Design the primary load-bearing chassis frame of a lunar-analog rover benchmark running the combined Mapping + Excavation + Science mission, supporting six rocker-bogie wheel modules, a front excavation tool mount, and a top-deck payload plate. Rover wet mass is capped at 90 kg.

Material class: 6061-T6 aluminum. No explicit standard is cited in the source; design to a 1.5 FoS on yield.

Envelope: stowed on the order of 1.2 m x 0.9 m x 0.8 m, payload deck ~0.5 m above the wheel plane.

Load cases.
- LC1 curb drop: 4 g vertical at the four outer wheel mounts with 90 kg supported.
- LC2 excavation reaction: 500 N horizontal + 200 N vertical at the tool-mount bolt pattern, six wheels pinned.
- LC3 slope: 1 g gravity at 30 deg pitch on 90 kg.
- LC4 lateral tip: 300 N at the top-deck CoM, 0.5 m above the wheel plane.

prompt.title: All-tasks lunar-analog rover primary chassis frame
prompt.brief: Load-bearing chassis for a 90 kg all-tasks lunar-analog rover performing Mapping, Excavation, and Science missions.

load_cases.name: slope_30deg
load_cases.magnitude: 1 g at 30 deg pitch on 90 kg

load_cases.name: lateral_tip
load_cases.application: top-deck CoM, 0.5 m above wheel plane
